english: Private Property of P.I. JokoDO NOT TOUCHDear Diary,Allow me to catch you up: Everyone in Kryta is an idiot and I hate it here.There's a twit of a princeling who can't seem to stop pulling the wings off of flies. Over and over—does he expect a different result? I'm sure he was dropped on his overly round head as a babe. Repeatedly.It's not as if the flies that survive have the capacity to spread rumors about his cruelty. What could possibly be the point of going back to that well? So shortsighted. So wasteful. So UNINSPIRED.Today, in the spirit of magnanimity, I pointed out a few creatures that might survive torture and be of use afterward. The coddled simpleton is surrounded by servants, ready to suffer and to even thank him for the attentions.To my utter horror he responded by engaging me in a contest of puns. It was atrocious and I couldn't extricate myself quickly enough for comfort.(...I may have actually met him in battle for several volleys. But that's between you and me. It's so boring here!)I will be overjoyed when this ordeal of Mother's diplomatic tours comes to an end.Yours,Iggy

Professor Drugga sent us to scout the forest west of our new operations base. It was a largely uneventful assignment—we didn't want to venture too far without backup—but when we started making our way back, my scanner would not cease beeping. It kept warning us about spikes of unidentified energies nearby, but we couldn't see, hear, or smell anything. I assumed the damn thing malfunctioned...until Yipp took a step forward and vanished. All that was left behind was a glittering ripple in the air and a single drawn-out scream. Kaysi and I lingered there for a while, mostly due to the shock, but we also observed numerous local creatures enter the same area and disappear as well.   Right before we headed back to base, Yipp emerged from the thicket drenched in sweat. He explained that after vanishing, he reappeared about a mile south of our location—spit out of a rift that opened up in the sky. We marked the location and headed right back to base to inform Drugga. She immediately escalated our findings to the director himself, who ordered us to build a small containment facility around the spot.    I wonder if we can use this to our advantage...

Progress is slow. We've harnessed the mountain's thermal springs for our furnaces, but we lack slave-cores for the Forged Colossus. The ice beasts assault our gates nightly, tearing them asunder, but their ranks seem to never thin no matter how many we fend off.The transformation is accelerating. The stone is now at my neck, and I feel the call to join the fight with our brethren. But I will resist—we will ALL resist. There is only one Summit.The scholars claim to understand enough about its magic to harness it, either to undo the Rite of the Great Dwarf or perhaps to leverage it. We could transform into something never before seen on the face of Tyria. We could also die.We have made our peace with death, and would welcome it over the life that the Deldrimor would have for us.

Project Omega: Initial Findings  by Head Researcher Skala    Overseer Kuda has tasked my krewe with the first round of experimentation for Project Omega. If you're reading this and unfamiliar with Project Omega, report yourself immediately to Compliance to initiate disciplinary proceedings!    **Introduction**  We've been ears-deep in research on the seer's magical infusion techniques for two solid weeks now—ever since our advanced team made the dangerous trek through the jungle. We're currently ensconced in the lab at Supra Fortis, attached to the forge on Artificer's Islet, where we've been studying the crafting processes of the elder species (particularly their creation of immensely powerful artifacts, such as Legavo and Ancora).    My krewe was given the immeasurable honor of adapting the seer infusion process to Overseer Kuda's own work with infusing advanced and volatile magics into a living subject. As this is our third generation of this work, initially pioneered by the overseer's father, Kudu, we were able to push past prototypes and into voluntary testing very quickly.    Our initial batch of candidates drew from a pool of thirty-two viable subjects from across various krewes. Some volunteered while others were assigned to the project; one such candidate was sent to the study krewe by Compliance. Only half of this initial batch made it through the preliminary screening process and received one dose of the exemplar serum.    **Exemplar Serum Results**  Unfortunately, our exemplar serum produced suboptimal results, with one-third of the initial testing group immediately succumbing to a rather gruesome and painful expiration. Surviving members of this test group appeared mostly unfazed by the serum's effects. We will continue to study the remains of the failed specimens and push forward with secondary testing ASAP.

Project Omega: Post-Mortem  by Head Researcher Skala    Director Kuda has taken full control over all Inquest operations in Eternity's Garden after former Director Vloxx supposedly turned against the Inquest during his operation at the Spire of Exaltation to the north of our position. Given the fluidity of the current situation, I am writing this report to serve both as my final report on Project Omega as well as my official letter of resignation from the Inquest, effective immediately.    **Conclusions**  Project Omega was, by the reckoning of our speculated project outcomes, a resounding success. Our three surviving subjects attained powers and abilities far beyond that of an ordinary asura. Yet, the project has been rife with red flags. From shoddy quality control and wasted time on redundant analyses to unclear objectives and goal setting from leadership across the board, Project Omega has been nothing short of a researcher's nightmare.    Director Kuda may not be pleased with these findings, but even she cannot deny that her three "champions" were neither well-suited nor even-tempered enough to handle the levels of power that they have been given.    Whoever finds this, give my regards to the director, but remind her that Skala is nobody's "underling."    I do not expect anyone will find me.

Project Omega: Preliminary Combat Report  by Head Researcher Skala, based on data gathered by Overseer Kuda    The three remaining subjects of Project Omega—now designated Omega Team—had their first field test in a real combat scenario where real-time data was collected both from external readings, taken in the field by Overseer Kuda herself, as well as from the subcutaneous sensors embedded within the subjects.    **Findings**  Initial readings indicate that all three members of Omega Team have continued to show marked improvement in physical, mental, and magical power and prowess. In some instances, such improvement has created unforeseen incidences of recklessness or acute episodes of excessive displays of their newfound abilities. Of particular note, the male subjects, Plokk and Zipp, appear to be more adversely impacted by these episodes than the sole female subject, Naati, though none are unaffected.     **Recommendations**  Further study seems warranted before sending the members of Omega Team into the field again. At the very least, they should not enter combat not without adequate support from this research krewe. Overseer Kuda has taken a more direct hand in their deployment, however. Consequently, Omega Team has failed to report in for routine observation and training and appear to be accompanying her in a more supportive capacity than this researcher believes is prudent. My recommendation is that they be reassigned to Supra Fortis for further study and monitoring in a controlled setting.

Proposals for Further Kralkatorrik Research.The excellent progress of our ancillary labs on Kralkatorrik's abilities has sparked a round of suggestions for further research. Formal proposals will be forthcoming, but consider this memo the first in a series of informal introductions to suggestions we've received from all of you.Our first proposal: Develop varied environmental enclosures for studying the effects of habitat on Kralkatorrik's minions.Pros: Inventive. Plausible; the fact that pre-Branded species have habitat preferences suggests the Branded may also show differential responses to environment.Cons: Minor cases of sweat rash from the prototype jungle environment.Look for another proposal next week!
